mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not exceed 55% when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals severe labor market distress that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in >50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle expansions confirms sustained manufacturing momentum, triggering short-term equity rallies over the next week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in >55% of late-cycle periods when INDPRO exceeds its trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month payroll increase exceeding 150,000 confirms robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumption, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following strong payroll prints.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal spending growth outpaces real GDP growth signals accelerating private sector demand relative to aggregate output, generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The spending-GDP divergence signal yields negative returns in >50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases during a tightening cycle compress equity multiples and trigger portfolio rebalancing away from risk assets, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly returns are positive in >60% of tightening cycle periods when rates rise.
